svg.text: Reso — Single-Agent Wellness Pipeline: End-to-End Workflow
LangGraph · Mem0 OSS (Qdrant + Neo4j + nomic-embed-text-v1.5, 768 dims) · FastAPI SSE · Supabase
LangGraph Pipeline — GraphState TypedDict · MemorySaver checkpointer
per-session thread_id → LangGraph replays conversation across requests
All 5 fetches run in parallel · asyncio.gather() · before agent executes
cache_get("profile:{user_id}") → DB fallback on miss → cache 300s TTL
① Profile (DB/cache) ② Memories from Mem0 ③ Graph Relations (Neo4j) ④ Safety Constraints ⑤ Today's Meals (with IDs) ⑥ Response Format
Example: "• User is vegetarian • No high-GI foods (Diabetes) • Breakfast: Oatmeal 300kcal [id: abc-123]"
① Build system prompt with all 6 context sections injected from GraphState
② Build message list: conversation_history pairs + current HumanMessage (text or multimodal)
Vector Search: Qdrant (nomic-embed-text-v1.5, 768 dims) → cosine similarity top-10
LLM Reranker (Feature 13): scores top-10 by wellness relevance → returns top-5
Graph Retrieval: Neo4j entity relationships (e.g., Diabetes → managed_with → Metformin)
→ Injected into system prompt as "What You Know About This User"
add_memory(fact, user_id, domain) · mem0_client.py:1525 · via save_memory_fact tool
Filters: only persistent health facts pass
Rejects: greetings, one-off statements, non-health facts
metadata: {domain, user_id, run_id(session_id)} for filtered retrieval
Mem0 OpenAI-compatible proxy · Qdrant only (no Neo4j graph)
Proxy auto-extracts facts via Feature 6/7 prompts
never delayed; failure is silently swallowed
Runs after agent response is sent → no latency impact
How a health fact gets stored (via Call #2 or Call #3)
User: "I'm vegetarian and allergic to peanuts"
"User is vegetarian" ✓ "User is allergic to peanuts" ✓
No existing memory → decision: ADD (both facts)
How memories reach the agent's system prompt (Call #1)
User: "What should I have for dinner?"
LLM Reranker (Feature 13 — llm_reranker) → scores by wellness relevance → top-5
System Prompt — "### What You Know About This User"
• User is vegetarian • User is allergic to peanuts
• User prefers spicy food • User eats dinner around 8pm
Token filter: event="on_chat_model_stream" AND "final_response" in tags — strips tool-call chunks, only real response tokens reach client
Fallback: if no tokens streamed (tool-only response) → chunk aggregated_response in 30-char segments
User is vegetarian · User allergic to peanuts
Always include macros when logging meals
Single LLM, All Tools: One ReAct loop with 13 tools handles nutrition + fitness + medical — simpler than multi-agent routing
Parallel Context Fetch: Profile, history, memories, meals all fetched with asyncio.gather before agent starts — latency = max(slowest), not sum
Metadata-Aware Retrieval: Domain keywords inferred from message → Qdrant metadata filter reduces irrelevant memory noise (Feature 2)
Wellness-Specific Memory: Custom prompts (Feature 6 extraction, Feature 7 reconciliation) ensure only persistent health facts are stored in Mem0
Dual Memory Path: Mem0 (Qdrant vector + Neo4j graph) for rich semantic retrieval; Supabase fallback for reliability; proxy for zero-friction auto-save
Non-Blocking Auto-Save: 15s timeout ensures streaming response is never delayed by background memory operations (Call #3)
Configurable OSS Stack: LLM, embedder, vector store, reranker all swappable via .env (Features 10–13) — no code change needed to switch providers
SSE Token Streaming: LLM tagged "final_response" → gateway strips tool-call chunks → real-time text to client with refresh_entities for UI updates
Example Value Injected into System Prompt
Reso Single-Agent Wellness Pipeline · src/agent/graph.py · src/infra/mem0_client.py · src/gateway/routes/chat.py
